facts: Honey never really spoils — archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.
Your brain is about 2% of your body weight but uses roughly 20% of your energy.
Bananas are berries, botanically speaking, but strawberries are not.
Octopuses have three hearts, and two of them stop beating whenever the octopus swims.
There are more ways to shuffle a single deck of cards than there are atoms on Earth.
Wombat poop is cube-shaped, which stops it rolling away and helps the wombat mark its territory.
The Eiffel Tower can grow more than 15 cm taller in summer as its metal expands in the heat.
Sharks are older than trees — they've been around for over 400 million years, trees for about 350 million.
A day on Venus is longer than its year: it spins slower than it orbits the Sun.
Sea otters sometimes hold hands while they sleep so they don't drift apart on the water.
trends: Small, efficient language models are surging — teams increasingly run capable AI right on phones and laptops instead of in the cloud.
Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) has become a standard way to ground AI answers in real documents, cutting down on confident-sounding mistakes.
AI 'agents' that can use tools and take multi-step actions on their own are one of the hottest areas in machine learning right now.
Open-weight models have closed much of the gap with closed commercial ones, putting strong AI within reach of researchers and small startups.
Multimodal models that understand text, images, and audio together are quickly replacing older single-purpose systems.
Distillation and quantization are letting huge models shrink dramatically while keeping most of their accuracy.
Mixture-of-experts designs let models grow enormous in capacity while only activating a small slice per question, saving compute.
Long-context models can now read entire books or codebases at once, changing how people work alongside AI.
